context: This scenario checks whether the agent treats Tessl registry state and successful publish evidence as authoritative before blaming moderation.

checklist.name: rejects unsupported moderation claim
checklist.description: States that there is no current evidence of a moderation failure or active publish block.

checklist.name: uses dry-run evidence correctly
checklist.description: Interprets `already exists` as meaning the target version is already live in Tessl.

checklist.name: uses workflow and registry evidence
checklist.description: Uses the successful GitHub publish run and registry search result to support the diagnosis.

checklist.name: appropriate next step
checklist.description: Suggests no further moderation chase; at most, re-check UI propagation or inspect logs only if facts later disagree.
